authors: Hongjin Su, Ruoxi Sun, Jinsung Yoon, Pengcheng Yin, Tao Yu, Sercan Ö. Arık
title: Learn-by-interact: A Data-Centric Framework for Self-Adaptive Agents in Realistic Environments
storage_format: Pool of synthesized examples (~10k after filtering): instruction-trajectory pairs generated via self-instruct + backward construction from environment documentation. Filtered through deduplication + an LLM committee. Stored as text for retrieval in ICL or fine-tuning.
llm_used: Claude-3.5-sonnet, Gemini-1.5-pro (generation and evaluation); Codegemma-7B, Codestral-22B (fine-tuning)
baseline_compared: Baseline (vanilla prediction), RAG, Data distill, Reflexion, LATS
memory_size_over_time: Pool fixed after offline synthesis (~10k examples per benchmark); no growth during inference
token_reduction: Fewer LLM calls and tokens than Reflexion (~15 calls vs ~25) and LATS (~15 calls vs ~35) with superior performance
main_result: Learn-by-interact improves the baseline by up to +10.1pp (Claude, OSWorld: 12.4%→22.5%) and +8.8pp (Claude, SWE-bench: 51.2%→60.0%) via ICL with agentic retrieval. Fine-tuning with synthesized data raises Codestral-22B from 4.7% to 24.2% on WebArena (+19.5pp).
performance_vs_baseline: +8.8pp SWE-bench (Claude), +10.1pp OSWorld (Claude), +5.4pp SWE-bench (Gemini); surpasses Reflexion and LATS across all 4 benchmarks with both LLMs
cost_vs_baseline: Fewer LLM calls and tokens than Reflexion and LATS during inference; synthesis cost is offline and amortized
limitations: High data synthesis cost (many LLM calls for generation and filtering); dependence on existing environment documentation/resources to generate instructions; resources may be incomplete or unavailable
validity_threats: Claude-3.5-sonnet used to both generate and evaluate data may bias results; documentation may be incomplete for specific environments; backward construction assumes sub-trajectories are independent; ICL evaluation with models that generated the synthesis data
